reasoning: Nvidia's stock threshold to end the year higher than 2024 is $134.29. The stock recently fell to around $115-$120 in late February / early March due to tariff fears and a muted reaction to their late February earnings. However, on March 12 and March 14, NVDA rallied roughly 6.4% and 5% respectively, putting the current price roughly around the $125-$130 mark (down only ~5-10% from the threshold). The stock has exactly 9.5 months to recover, and strong upcoming catalysts — such as the GTC conference next week — alongside general market strength suggest it can relatively easily bridge the gap. Still, a $134.29 target requires an approximate 5-8% increase from today's price just to break even, and Nvidia's high volatility could act in either direction. I assess the probability to be around 65%, aligned with the historical base rate of mega-cap tech stocks posting positive annual returns, while tempering the slightly more optimistic community consensus (67% pre-rally) with the reality of its current year-to-date underwater position.
